playit.name: playit.gg
playit.description: Free tunnel provider, no API key required. Easy to set up, good for casual use.
setupSteps: Download playit.gg from [https://playit.gg/download](https://playit.gg/download)
Extract the downloaded ZIP file
Run the playit.exe application
Follow the on-screen setup wizard
Select your Minecraft server from the list
Copy the generated IP address and port
In Nexora Launcher, go to Settings → Tunnel providers
Enter the playit.exe path in the Playit executable field
Click 'Save tunnel settings'
You can now start tunnels from the Tunnel page
playit.notes: Playit.gg is completely free and doesn't require an account or API key. The generated IP may change each time you restart the tunnel.

ngrok.name: ngrok
ngrok.description: Popular tunnel provider with stable IPs. Requires free account and API key.
setupSteps: Create a free account at [https://dashboard.ngrok.com/signup](https://dashboard.ngrok.com/signup)
After signing up, go to [https://dashboard.ngrok.com/get-started/your-authtoken](https://dashboard.ngrok.com/get-started/your-authtoken)
Copy your authtoken (it looks like: 2abc...xyz)
Download ngrok from [https://ngrok.com/download](https://ngrok.com/download)
Extract the downloaded ZIP file
In Nexora Launcher, go to Settings → Tunnel providers
Paste your authtoken in the Ngrok API key field
Enter the ngrok.exe path in the Ngrok executable field
Click 'Save tunnel settings'
You can now start tunnels from the Tunnel page
ngrok.notes: Ngrok provides a stable IP address that doesn't change. The free tier has limitations on connection time and bandwidth.

frp.name: frp (Fast Reverse Proxy)
frp.description: Open-source tunnel solution. Requires a server or a service like Cloudflare Tunnel.
setupSteps: Download frp from [https://github.com/fatedier/frp/releases](https://github.com/fatedier/frp/releases)
Extract the downloaded ZIP file
You need a frp server or use a service like Cloudflare Tunnel
Configure frps.ini (server) and frpc.ini (client) configuration files
In Nexora Launcher, go to Settings → Tunnel providers
Enter the frpc.exe path in the Frp executable field
If using a service with an API key, enter it in the Frp API key field
Click 'Save tunnel settings'
You can now start tunnels from the Tunnel page
frp.notes: FRP is more complex to set up than playit.gg or ngrok. It's recommended for advanced users who have their own server.

bore.name: bore.pub
bore.description: Simple, free tunnel provider. No API key required. Minimal setup.
setupSteps: Download bore from [https://github.com/ekzhang/bore/releases](https://github.com/ekzhang/bore/releases)
Extract the downloaded ZIP file
In Nexora Launcher, go to Settings → Tunnel providers
Enter the bore.exe path in the Bore executable field
Click 'Save tunnel settings'
You can now start tunnels from the Tunnel page
bore.notes: Bore is very simple to use but may have less stable connections compared to ngrok. It's completely free.

generalTunnelInfo.whatIsTunneling: Tunneling allows you to make your local Minecraft server accessible over the internet without port forwarding. It creates a secure tunnel from your computer to a public server.
whenToUse: You don't have access to router port forwarding
You're on a restricted network (school, work)
You want a quick way to share your server with friends
You don't have a static public IP address
securityNotes: Always use strong passwords on your Minecraft server
Be cautious about who you share your tunnel IP with
Free tunnel providers may have bandwidth limitations
Tunneling is generally safe but always use reputable providers

troubleshooting.connectionFailed: Check that the tunnel provider executable path is correct in Settings. Make sure your Minecraft server is actually running before starting the tunnel.
troubleshooting.ipNotWorking: Wait a few seconds after starting the tunnel. Some providers need time to establish the connection. Try refreshing the tunnel status.
troubleshooting.portAlreadyInUse: Another application may be using the port. Try a different port in your server settings.

linkFormat: When providing links in your response, always use markdown format like this: [link text](https://example.com). This makes links clickable in the UI.
